#### Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.Filters`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/transaction_filter.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/transaction_filter.proto#L15)
- Fields: 1
- Nested messages: 0
- Nested enums: 0

The union of a set of template filters, interface filters, or a wildcard.

| # | Field | Type | Label | Oneof | Description | Metadata |
| -: | :---- | :--- | :---- | :---- | :---------- | :------- |
| 1 | `cumulative` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.CumulativeFilter` | repeated |  | Every filter in the cumulative list expands the scope of the resulting stream. Each interface,<br/> template or wildcard filter means additional events that will match the query.<br/> The impact of include_interface_view and include_created_event_blob fields in the filters will<br/> also be accumulated.<br/> A template or an interface SHOULD NOT appear twice in the accumulative field.<br/> A wildcard filter SHOULD NOT be defined more than once in the accumulative field.<br/> Optional, if no ``CumulativeFilter`` defined, the default of a single ``WildcardFilter`` with<br/> include_created_event_blob unset is used. |  |

#### Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.CumulativeFilter`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/transaction_filter.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/transaction_filter.proto#L29)
- Fields: 3
- Nested messages: 0
- Nested enums: 0

A filter that matches all contracts that are either an instance of one of
 the ``template_filters`` or that match one of the ``interface_filters``.

Oneofs:
- `identifier_filter` (3 fields)

| # | Field | Type | Label | Oneof | Description | Metadata |
| -: | :---- | :--- | :---- | :---- | :---------- | :------- |
| 1 | `wildcard_filter` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.WildcardFilter` | optional | `identifier_filter` | A wildcard filter that matches all templates<br/> Optional |  |
| 2 | `interface_filter` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.InterfaceFilter` | optional | `identifier_filter` | Include an ``InterfaceView`` for every ``InterfaceFilter`` matching a contract.<br/> The ``InterfaceFilter`` instances MUST each use a unique ``interface_id``.<br/> Optional |  |
| 3 | `template_filter` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.TemplateFilter` | optional | `identifier_filter` | A template for which the data will be included in the<br/> ``create_arguments`` of a matching ``CreatedEvent``.<br/> If a contract is simultaneously selected by a template filter and one or more interface filters,<br/> the corresponding ``include_created_event_blob`` are consolidated using an OR operation.<br/> Optional |  |

#### Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.WildcardFilter`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/transaction_filter.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/transaction_filter.proto#L50)
- Fields: 1
- Nested messages: 0
- Nested enums: 0

This filter matches all templates.

| # | Field | Type | Label | Oneof | Description | Metadata |
| -: | :---- | :--- | :---- | :---- | :---------- | :------- |
| 1 | `include_created_event_blob` | `bool` | optional |  | Whether to include a ``created_event_blob`` in the returned ``CreatedEvent``.<br/> Use this to access the contract create event payload in your API client<br/> for submitting it as a disclosed contract with future commands.<br/> Optional |  |

#### Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.InterfaceFilter`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/transaction_filter.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/transaction_filter.proto#L59)
- Fields: 3
- Nested messages: 0
- Nested enums: 0

This filter matches contracts that implement a specific interface.

| # | Field | Type | Label | Oneof | Description | Metadata |
| -: | :---- | :--- | :---- | :---- | :---------- | :------- |
| 1 | `interface_id` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.Identifier` | optional |  | The interface that a matching contract must implement.<br/> The ``interface_id`` needs to be valid: corresponding interface should be defined in<br/> one of the available packages at the time of the query.<br/> Both package-name and package-id reference formats for the identifier are supported.<br/> Note: The package-id reference identifier format is deprecated. We plan to end support for this format in version 3.4.<br/><br/> Required |  |
| 2 | `include_interface_view` | `bool` | optional |  | Whether to include the interface view on the contract in the returned ``CreatedEvent``.<br/> Use this to access contract data in a uniform manner in your API client.<br/> Optional |  |
| 3 | `include_created_event_blob` | `bool` | optional |  | Whether to include a ``created_event_blob`` in the returned ``CreatedEvent``.<br/> Use this to access the contract create event payload in your API client<br/> for submitting it as a disclosed contract with future commands.<br/> Optional |  |

#### Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.TemplateFilter`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/transaction_filter.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/transaction_filter.proto#L82)
- Fields: 2
- Nested messages: 0
- Nested enums: 0

This filter matches contracts of a specific template.

| # | Field | Type | Label | Oneof | Description | Metadata |
| -: | :---- | :--- | :---- | :---- | :---------- | :------- |
| 1 | `template_id` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.Identifier` | optional |  | A template for which the payload should be included in the response.<br/> The ``template_id`` needs to be valid: corresponding template should be defined in<br/> one of the available packages at the time of the query.<br/> Both package-name and package-id reference formats for the identifier are supported.<br/> Note: The package-id reference identifier format is deprecated. We plan to end support for this format in version 3.4.<br/><br/> Required |  |
| 2 | `include_created_event_blob` | `bool` | optional |  | Whether to include a ``created_event_blob`` in the returned ``CreatedEvent``.<br/> Use this to access the contract event payload in your API client<br/> for submitting it as a disclosed contract with future commands.<br/> Optional |  |

#### Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.EventFormat`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/transaction_filter.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/transaction_filter.proto#L104)
- Fields: 3
- Nested messages: 0
- Nested enums: 0

A format for events which defines both which events should be included
 and what data should be computed and included for them.

 Note that some of the filtering behavior depends on the `TransactionShape`,
 which is expected to be specified alongside usages of `EventFormat`.

| # | Field | Type | Label | Oneof | Description | Metadata |
| -: | :---- | :--- | :---- | :---- | :---------- | :------- |
| 1 | `filters_by_party` | `map<string, com.daml.ledger.api.v2.Filters>` | repeated |  | Each key must be a valid PartyIdString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> The interpretation of the filter depends on the transaction-shape being filtered:<br/><br/> 1. For **ledger-effects** create and exercise events are returned, for which the witnesses include at least one of<br/>    the listed parties and match the per-party filter.<br/> 2. For **transaction and active-contract-set streams** create and archive events are returned for all contracts whose<br/>    stakeholders include at least one of the listed parties and match the per-party filter.<br/><br/> Optional |  |
| 2 | `filters_for_any_party` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.Filters` | optional |  | Wildcard filters that apply to all the parties existing on the participant. The interpretation of the filters is the same<br/> with the per-party filter as described above.<br/> Optional |  |
| 3 | `verbose` | `bool` | optional |  | If enabled, values served over the API will contain more information than strictly necessary to interpret the data.<br/> In particular, setting the verbose flag to true triggers the ledger to include labels for record fields.<br/> Optional |  |

#### Enum `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.TransactionShape`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/transaction_filter.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/transaction_filter.proto#L129)
- Values: 3

Event shape for Transactions.
 Shapes are exclusive and only one of them can be defined in queries.

| Name | Number | Description | Metadata |
| :--- | -----: | :---------- | :------- |
| `TRANSACTION_SHAPE_UNSPECIFIED` | 0 | Following official proto3 convention, not intended for actual use. |  |
| `TRANSACTION_SHAPE_ACS_DELTA` | 1 | Transaction shape that is sufficient to maintain an accurate ACS view.<br/> The field witness_parties in events are populated as stakeholders, transaction filter will apply accordingly.<br/> This translates to create and archive events. |  |
| `TRANSACTION_SHAPE_LEDGER_EFFECTS` | 2 | Transaction shape that allows maintaining an ACS and also conveys detailed information about<br/> all exercises.<br/> The field witness_parties in events are populated as cumulative informees, transaction filter will apply accordingly.<br/> This translates to create, consuming exercise and non-consuming exercise. |  |

A format that specifies what events to include in Daml transactions
 and what data to compute and include for them.

| -: | :---- | :--- | :---- | :---- | :---------- | :------- |
| 1 | `event_format` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.EventFormat` | optional |  | Required |  |
| 2 | `transaction_shape` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.TransactionShape` | optional |  | What transaction shape to use for interpreting the filters of the event format.<br/> Required |  |

A format specifying which topology transactions to include and how to render them.

| # | Field | Type | Label | Oneof | Description | Metadata |
| -: | :---- | :--- | :---- | :---- | :---------- | :------- |
| 1 | `include_participant_authorization_events` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.ParticipantAuthorizationTopologyFormat` | optional |  | Include participant authorization topology events in streams.<br/> Optional, if unset no participant authorization topology events are emitted in the stream. |  |

#### Service `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.UpdateService`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/update_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/update_service.proto#L30)
- Endpoints: 3

Allows clients to read updates (transactions, (un)assignments, topology events) from the ledger.

 ``GetUpdates`` and ``GetUpdateTrees`` provide a comprehensive stream of updates/changes
 which happened on the virtual shared ledger. These streams are indexed with ledger
 offsets, which are strictly increasing.
 The virtual shared ledger consist of changes happening on multiple synchronizers which are
 connected to the serving participant. Each update belongs to one synchronizer, this is
 provided in the result (the ``synchronizer_id`` field in ``Transaction``
 for transactions, the ``source`` field in ``UnassignedEvent`` and the ``target`` field in ``AssignedEvent``).
 Consumers can rely on strong causal guarantees on the virtual shared ledger for a single
 synchronizer: updates which have greater offsets are happened after than updates with smaller
 offsets for the same synchronizer. Across different synchronizers this is not guaranteed.

##### Endpoint `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.UpdateService/GetUpdates`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/update_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/update_service.proto#L37)
- Endpoint page: [reference](/docs-main/appdev/reference/protobuf-history/endpoints/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/updateservice/getupdates/index)
- Request: `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.GetUpdatesRequest`
- Response: `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.GetUpdatesResponse`
- Client streaming: no
- Server streaming: yes

Read the ledger's filtered update stream for the specified contents and filters.
 It returns the event types in accordance with the stream contents selected. Also the selection criteria
 for individual events depends on the transaction shape chosen.

 - ACS delta: a requesting party must be a stakeholder of an event for it to be included.
 - ledger effects: a requesting party must be a witness of an en event for it to be included.

##### Endpoint `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.UpdateService/GetUpdateByOffset`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/update_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/update_service.proto#L41)
- Endpoint page: [reference](/docs-main/appdev/reference/protobuf-history/endpoints/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/updateservice/getupdatebyoffset/index)
- Request: `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.GetUpdateByOffsetRequest`
- Response: `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.GetUpdateResponse`
- Client streaming: no
- Server streaming: no

Lookup an update by its offset.
 If there is no update with this offset, or all the events are filtered, an UPDATE_NOT_FOUND error will be raised.

- Response: `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.GetUpdateResponse`
- Client streaming: no
- Server streaming: no

Lookup an update by its ID.
 If there is no update with this ID, or all the events are filtered, an UPDATE_NOT_FOUND error will be raised.

#### Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.GetUpdatesRequest`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/update_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/update_service.proto#L48)
- Fields: 3
- Nested messages: 0
- Nested enums: 0
- Reserved ranges: `[{"start": 3, "end": 4}, {"start": 4, "end": 5}]`
- Reserved names: `["filter", "verbose"]`

_No description._

| # | Field | Type | Label | Oneof | Description | Metadata |
| -: | :---- | :--- | :---- | :---- | :---------- | :------- |
| 1 | `begin_exclusive` | `int64` | optional |  | Beginning of the requested ledger section (non-negative integer).<br/> The response will only contain transactions whose offset is strictly greater than this.<br/> If zero, the stream will start from the beginning of the ledger.<br/> If positive, the streaming will start after this absolute offset.<br/> If the ledger has been pruned, this parameter must be specified and be greater than the pruning offset. |  |
| 2 | `end_inclusive` | `int64` | optional |  | End of the requested ledger section.<br/> The response will only contain transactions whose offset is less than or equal to this.<br/> Optional, if empty, the stream will not terminate.<br/> If specified, the stream will terminate after this absolute offset (positive integer) is reached. |  |
| 5 | `update_format` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.UpdateFormat` | optional |  | Must be unset for GetUpdateTrees request.<br/> Optional for backwards compatibility for GetUpdates request: defaults to an UpdateFormat where:<br/><br/> - include_transactions.event_format.filters_by_party = the filter.filters_by_party on this request<br/> - include_transactions.event_format.filters_for_any_party = the filter.filters_for_any_party on this request<br/> - include_transactions.event_format.verbose = the same flag specified on this request<br/> - include_transactions.transaction_shape = TRANSACTION_SHAPE_ACS_DELTA<br/> - include_reassignments.filter = the same filter specified on this request<br/> - include_reassignments.verbose = the same flag specified on this request<br/> - include_topology_events.include_participant_authorization_events.parties = all the parties specified in filter |  |

Status: experimental interface, will change before it is deemed production
 ready

 The inspection service provides methods for the ledger administrator
 to look under the hood of a running system.
 In V2 Ledger API this service is not available.

##### Endpoint `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.CommandInspectionService/GetCommandStatus`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/command_inspection_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/command_inspection_service.proto#L29)
- Endpoint page: [reference](/docs-main/appdev/reference/protobuf-history/endpoints/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/commandinspectionservice/getcommandstatus/index)
- Request: `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.GetCommandStatusRequest`
- Response: `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.GetCommandStatusResponse`
- Client streaming: no
- Server streaming: no

Inquire about the status of a command.
 This service is used for debugging only. The command status is only tracked in memory and is not persisted.
 The service can be used to understand the failure status and the structure of a command.
 Requires admin privileges
 The service is alpha without backward compatibility guarantees.

#### Service `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.IdentityProviderConfigService`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/identity_provider_config_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/identity_provider_config_service.proto#L37)
- Endpoints: 5

Identity Provider Config Service makes it possible for participant node administrators
 to setup and manage additional identity providers at runtime.

 This allows using access tokens from identity providers unknown at deployment time. When an identity
 provider is configured, independent IDP administrators can manage their own set of parties and users.
 Such parties and users have a matching `identity_provider_id` defined and are inaccessible to
 administrators from other identity providers. A user will only be authenticated if the corresponding JWT
 token is issued by the appropriate identity provider.
 Users and parties without `identity_provider_id` defined are assumed to be using the default identity provider,
 which is configured statically at the participant node's deployment time.

 The Ledger API uses the "iss" claim of a JWT token to match the token to a specific IDP. If there is no match,
 the default IDP is assumed.

 The fields of request messages (and sub-messages) are marked either as ``Optional`` or ``Required``:

 1. ``Optional`` denoting the client may leave the field unset when sending a request.
 2. ``Required`` denoting the client must set the field to a non-default value when sending a request.

 An identity provider config resource is described by the ``IdentityProviderConfig`` message,
 An identity provider config resource, once it has been created, can be modified.
 In order to update the properties represented by the ``IdentityProviderConfig`` message use the ``UpdateIdentityProviderConfig`` RPC.
 The only fields that can be modified are those marked as ``Modifiable``.

| 2 | `is_deactivated` | `bool` | optional |  | When set, the callers using JWT tokens issued by this identity provider are denied all access<br/> to the Ledger API.<br/> Optional,<br/> Modifiable |  |
| 3 | `issuer` | `string` | optional |  | Specifies the issuer of the JWT token.<br/> The issuer value is a case sensitive URL using the https scheme that contains scheme, host,<br/> and optionally, port number and path components and no query or fragment components.<br/> Required<br/> Modifiable |  |
| 4 | `jwks_url` | `string` | optional |  | The JWKS (JSON Web Key Set) URL.<br/> The Ledger API uses JWKs (JSON Web Keys) from the provided URL to verify that the JWT has been<br/> signed with the loaded JWK. Only RS256 (RSA Signature with SHA-256) signing algorithm is supported.<br/> Required<br/> Modifiable |  |
| 5 | `audience` | `string` | optional |  | Specifies the audience of the JWT token.<br/> When set, the callers using JWT tokens issued by this identity provider are allowed to get an access<br/> only if the "aud" claim includes the string specified here<br/> Optional,<br/> Modifiable |  |

 Based on ``ObjectMeta`` meta used in Kubernetes API.
 See https://github.com/kubernetes/apimachinery/blob/master/pkg/apis/meta/v1/generated.proto#L640

#### Service `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.PackageManagementService`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/package_management_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/package_management_service.proto#L21)
- Endpoints: 4

Status: experimental interface, will change before it is deemed production
 ready

 Query the Daml-LF packages supported by the ledger participant and upload
 DAR files. We use 'backing participant' to refer to this specific participant
 in the methods of this API.

##### Endpoint `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.PackageManagementService/ListKnownPackages`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/package_management_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/package_management_service.proto#L23)
- Endpoint page: [reference](/docs-main/appdev/reference/protobuf-history/endpoints/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/packagemanagementservice/listknownpackages/index)
- Request: `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.ListKnownPackagesRequest`
- Response: `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.ListKnownPackagesResponse`
- Client streaming: no
- Server streaming: no

Returns the details of all Daml-LF packages known to the backing participant.

##### Endpoint `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.PackageManagementService/UploadDarFile`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/package_management_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/package_management_service.proto#L30)
- Endpoint page: [reference](/docs-main/appdev/reference/protobuf-history/endpoints/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/packagemanagementservice/uploaddarfile/index)
- Request: `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.UploadDarFileRequest`
- Response: `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.UploadDarFileResponse`
- Client streaming: no
- Server streaming: no

Upload a DAR file to the participant.

 If vetting is enabled in the request, the DAR is checked for upgrade compatibility
 with the set of the already vetted packages on the target vetting synchronizer
 See UploadDarFileRequest for details regarding vetting and the target vetting synchronizer.

##### Endpoint `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.PackageManagementService/ValidateDarFile`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/package_management_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/package_management_service.proto#L38)
- Endpoint page: [reference](/docs-main/appdev/reference/protobuf-history/endpoints/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/packagemanagementservice/validatedarfile/index)
- Request: `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.ValidateDarFileRequest`
- Response: `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.ValidateDarFileResponse`
- Client streaming: no
- Server streaming: no

Validates the DAR and checks the upgrade compatibility of the DAR's packages
 with the set of the already vetted packages on the target vetting synchronizer.
 See ValidateDarFileRequest for details regarding the target vetting synchronizer.

 The operation has no effect on the state of the participant or the Canton ledger:
 the DAR payload and its packages are not persisted neither are the packages vetted.

| 2 | `submission_id` | `string` | optional |  | Unique submission identifier.<br/> Optional, defaults to a random identifier. |  |
| 3 | `vetting_change` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.UploadDarFileRequest.VettingChange` | optional |  | How to vet packages in the DAR being uploaded |  |
| 4 | `synchronizer_id` | `string` | optional |  | Only used if VettingChange is set to VETTING_CHANGE_VET_ALL_PACKAGES, in<br/> order to specify which synchronizer to vet on.<br/><br/> If synchronizer_id is set, the synchronizer with this ID will be used. If<br/> synchronizer_id is unset and the participant is only connected to a single<br/> synchronizer, that synchronizer will be used by default. If synchronizer_id<br/> is unset and the participant is connected to multiple synchronizers, the<br/> request will error out with PACKAGE_SERVICE_CANNOT_AUTODETECT_SYNCHRONIZER.<br/><br/> Optional |  |

- Fields: 3
- Nested messages: 0
- Nested enums: 0

Set vetting bounds of a list of packages. Packages that were not previously
 vetted have their bounds added, previous vetting bounds are overwritten.

| # | Field | Type | Label | Oneof | Description | Metadata |
| -: | :---- | :--- | :---- | :---- | :---------- | :------- |
| 1 | `packages` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.VettedPackagesRef` | repeated |  | Packages to be vetted.<br/><br/> If a reference in this list matches more than one package, the change is<br/> considered ambiguous and the entire update request is rejected. In other<br/> words, every reference must match exactly one package. |  |
| 2 | `new_valid_from_inclusive` | `google.protobuf.Timestamp` | optional |  | The time from which these packages should be vetted, prior lower bounds<br/> are overwritten.<br/> Optional |  |
| 3 | `new_valid_until_exclusive` | `google.protobuf.Timestamp` | optional |  | The time until which these packages should be vetted, prior upper bounds<br/> are overwritten.<br/> Optional |  |

#### Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.VettedPackagesRef`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/package_management_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/package_management_service.proto#L190)
- Fields: 3
- Nested messages: 0
- Nested enums: 0

A reference to identify one or more packages.

 A reference matches a package if its ``package_id`` matches the package's ID,
 its ``package_name`` matches the package's name, and its ``package_version``
 matches the package's version. If an attribute in the reference is left
 unspecified (i.e. as an empty string), that attribute is treated as a
 wildcard. At a minimum, ``package_id`` or the ``package_name`` must be
 specified.

 If a reference does not match any package, the reference is considered
 unresolved and the entire update request is rejected.

| # | Field | Type | Label | Oneof | Description | Metadata |
| -: | :---- | :--- | :---- | :---- | :---------- | :------- |
| 1 | `package_id` | `string` | optional |  | Package's package id must be the same as this field.<br/> Optional |  |
| 2 | `package_name` | `string` | optional |  | Package's name must be the same as this field.<br/> Optional |  |
| 3 | `package_version` | `string` | optional |  | Package's version must be the same as this field.<br/> Optional |  |

#### Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.UpdateVettedPackagesRequest`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/package_management_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/package_management_service.proto#L204)
- Fields: 5
- Nested messages: 0
- Nested enums: 0

_No description._

| # | Field | Type | Label | Oneof | Description | Metadata |
| -: | :---- | :--- | :---- | :---- | :---------- | :------- |
| 1 | `changes` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.VettedPackagesChange` | repeated |  | Changes to apply to the current vetting state of the participant on the<br/> specified synchronizer. The changes are applied in order.<br/> Any package not changed will keep their previous vetting state. |  |
| 2 | `dry_run` | `bool` | optional |  | If dry_run is true, then the changes are only prepared, but not applied. If<br/> a request would trigger an error when run (e.g. TOPOLOGY_DEPENDENCIES_NOT_VETTED),<br/> it will also trigger an error when dry_run.<br/><br/> Use this flag to preview a change before applying it. |  |
| 3 | `synchronizer_id` | `string` | optional |  | If set, the requested changes will take place on the specified<br/> synchronizer. If synchronizer_id is unset and the participant is only<br/> connected to a single synchronizer, that synchronizer will be used by<br/> default. If synchronizer_id is unset and the participant is connected to<br/> multiple synchronizers, the request will error out with<br/> PACKAGE_SERVICE_CANNOT_AUTODETECT_SYNCHRONIZER.<br/><br/> Optional |  |
| 4 | `expected_topology_serial` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.PriorTopologySerial` | optional |  | The serial of the last ``VettedPackages`` topology transaction of this<br/> participant and on this synchronizer.<br/><br/> Execution of the request fails if this is not correct. Use this to guard<br/> against concurrent changes.<br/><br/> If left unspecified, no validation is done against the last transaction's<br/> serial.<br/><br/> Optional |  |
| 5 | `update_vetted_packages_force_flags` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.UpdateVettedPackagesForceFlag` | repeated |  | Controls whether potentially unsafe vetting updates are allowed.<br/><br/> Optional, defaults to FORCE_FLAG_UNSPECIFIED. |  |

| :--- | -----: | :---------- | :------- |

| `UPDATE_VETTED_PACKAGES_FORCE_FLAG_ALLOW_UNVETTED_DEPENDENCIES` | 3 | * Allow vetting a package without vetting one or more of its dependencies |  |

#### Service `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.ParticipantPruningService`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/participant_pruning_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/participant_pruning_service.proto#L23)
- Endpoints: 1

Status: experimental interface, will change before it is deemed production
 ready

Prunes/truncates the "oldest" transactions from the participant (the participant Ledger Api Server plus any other
 participant-local state) by removing a portion of the ledger in such a way that the set of future, allowed
 commands are not affected.

 This enables:

 1. keeping the "inactive" portion of the ledger to a manageable size and
 2. removing inactive state to honor the right to be forgotten.

##### Endpoint `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.ParticipantPruningService/Prune`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/participant_pruning_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/participant_pruning_service.proto#L26)

- Request: `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.PruneRequest`
- Response: `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.PruneResponse`
- Client streaming: no
- Server streaming: no

Prune the ledger specifying the offset before and at which ledger transactions should be removed. Only returns when
 the potentially long-running prune request ends successfully or with an error.

| 1 | `prune_up_to` | `int64` | optional |  | Inclusive valid absolute offset (positive integer) up to which the ledger is to be pruned.<br/> By default the following data is pruned:<br/><br/> 1. All normal and divulged contracts that have been archived before<br/>    `prune_up_to`.<br/> 2. All transaction events and completions before `prune_up_to`<br/> 3. All immediately divulged contracts created before `prune_up_to` independent of whether<br/>    they were archived before `prune_up_to`. |  |
| 2 | `submission_id` | `string` | optional |  | Unique submission identifier.<br/> Optional, defaults to a random identifier, used for logging. |  |
| 3 | `prune_all_divulged_contracts` | `bool` | optional |  | Deprecated flag used to prune all immediately and retroactively divulged contracts.<br/> It is a no-op since the divulged contracts are pruned along with the deactivated contracts. |  |

#### Service `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.PartyManagementService`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/party_management_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/party_management_service.proto#L37)
- Endpoints: 8

This service allows inspecting the party management state of the ledger known to the participant
 and managing the participant-local party metadata.

 The authorization rules for its RPCs are specified on the ``&lt;RpcName&gt;Request``
 messages as boolean expressions over these facts:

 1. ``HasRight(r)`` denoting whether the authenticated user has right ``r`` and
 2. ``IsAuthenticatedIdentityProviderAdmin(idp)`` denoting whether ``idp`` is equal to the ``identity_provider_id``
    of the authenticated user and the user has an IdentityProviderAdmin right.

 If `identity_provider_id` is set to an empty string, then it's effectively set to the value of access token's 'iss' field if that is provided.
 If `identity_provider_id` remains an empty string, the default identity provider will be assumed.

 The fields of request messages (and sub-messages) are marked either as ``Optional`` or ``Required``:

 1. ``Optional`` denoting the client may leave the field unset when sending a request.
 2. ``Required`` denoting the client must set the field to a non-default value when sending a request.

 A party details resource is described by the ``PartyDetails`` message,
 A party details resource, once it has been created, can be modified using the ``UpdatePartyDetails`` RPC.
 The only fields that can be modified are those marked as ``Modifiable``.

##### Endpoint `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.PartyManagementService/GetParticipantId`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/party_management_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/party_management_service.proto#L42)

Return the identifier of the participant.
 All horizontally scaled replicas should return the same id.
 daml-on-kv-ledger: returns an identifier supplied on command line at launch time
 canton: returns globally unique identifier of the participant

##### Endpoint `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.PartyManagementService/AllocateParty`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/party_management_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/party_management_service.proto#L70)
- Endpoint page: [reference](/docs-main/appdev/reference/protobuf-history/endpoints/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/partymanagementservice/allocateparty/index)
- Request: `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.AllocatePartyRequest`
- Response: `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.AllocatePartyResponse`
- Client streaming: no
- Server streaming: no

Allocates a new party on a ledger and adds it to the set managed by the participant.
 Caller specifies a party identifier suggestion, the actual identifier
 allocated might be different and is implementation specific.
 Caller can specify party metadata that is stored locally on the participant.
 This call may:

 - Succeed, in which case the actual allocated identifier is visible in
   the response.
 - Respond with a gRPC error

 daml-on-kv-ledger: suggestion's uniqueness is checked by the validators in
 the consensus layer and call rejected if the identifier is already present.
 canton: completely different globally unique identifier is allocated.
 Behind the scenes calls to an internal protocol are made. As that protocol
 is richer than the surface protocol, the arguments take implicit values
 The party identifier suggestion must be a valid party name. Party names are required to be non-empty US-ASCII strings built from letters, digits, space,
 colon, minus and underscore limited to 255 chars

##### Endpoint `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.PartyManagementService/AllocateExternalParty`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/party_management_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/party_management_service.proto#L85)
- Endpoint page: [reference](/docs-main/appdev/reference/protobuf-history/endpoints/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/partymanagementservice/allocateexternalparty/index)
- Request: `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.AllocateExternalPartyRequest`
- Response: `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.AllocateExternalPartyResponse`
- Client streaming: no
- Server streaming: no

Alpha 3.3: Endpoint to allocate a new external party on a synchronizer

 Expected to be stable in 3.5

 The external party must be hosted (at least) on this node with either confirmation or observation permissions
 It can optionally be hosted on other nodes (then called a multi-hosted party).
 If hosted on additional nodes, explicit authorization of the hosting relationship must be performed on those nodes
 before the party can be used.
 Decentralized namespaces are supported but must be provided fully authorized by their owners.
 The individual owner namespace transactions can be submitted in the same call (fully authorized as well).
 In the simple case of a non-multi hosted, non-decentralized party, the RPC will return once the party is
 effectively allocated and ready to use, similarly to the AllocateParty behavior.
 For more complex scenarios applications may need to query the party status explicitly (only through the admin API as of now).

##### Endpoint `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.PartyManagementService/GenerateExternalPartyTopology`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/party_management_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/party_management_service.proto#L104)

- Response: `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.GenerateExternalPartyTopologyResponse`
- Client streaming: no
- Server streaming: no

Alpha 3.3: Convenience endpoint to generate topology transactions for external signing

 Expected to be stable in 3.5

 You may use this endpoint to generate the common external topology transactions
 which can be signed externally and uploaded as part of the allocate party process

 Note that this request will create a normal namespace using the same key for the
 identity as for signing. More elaborate schemes such as multi-signature
 or decentralized parties require you to construct the topology transactions yourself.

| # | Field | Type | Label | Oneof | Description | Metadata |
| -: | :---- | :--- | :---- | :---- | :---------- | :------- |
| 2 | `page_token` | `string` | optional |  | Pagination token to determine the specific page to fetch. Using the token guarantees that parties on a subsequent<br/> page are all lexically greater than the last party on a previous page. Server does not store intermediate results<br/> between calls chained by a series of page tokens. As a consequence, if new parties are being added and a page is<br/> requested twice using the same token, more parties can be returned on the second call.<br/> Leave empty to fetch the first page.<br/> Optional |  |
| 3 | `page_size` | `int32` | optional |  | Maximum number of results to be returned by the server. The server will return no more than that many results,<br/> but it might return fewer. If the page_size is 0, the server will decide the number of results to be returned.<br/> If the page_size exceeds the maximum supported by the server, an error will be returned. To obtain the server's<br/> maximum consult the PartyManagementFeature descriptor available in the VersionService.<br/> Optional |  |
| 1 | `identity_provider_id` | `string` | optional |  | The id of the ``Identity Provider`` whose parties should be retrieved.<br/> Optional, if not set, assume the party is managed by the default identity provider or party is not hosted by the participant. |  |
| 4 | `filter_party` | `string` | optional |  | An optional filter for the party name, searching for all party names known to this node<br/> starting with the given prefix. This can either be just a string or extend up to the full<br/> identifier.<br/> Optional |  |

| # | Field | Type | Label | Oneof | Description | Metadata |
| -: | :---- | :--- | :---- | :---- | :---------- | :------- |
| 1 | `synchronizer` | `string` | optional |  | TODO(#27670) support synchronizer aliases<br/> Synchronizer ID on which to onboard the party<br/> Required |  |
| 2 | `onboarding_transactions` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.AllocateExternalPartyRequest.SignedTransaction` | repeated |  | TopologyTransactions to onboard the external party<br/> Can contain:<br/> - A namespace for the party.<br/> This can be either a single NamespaceDelegation,<br/> or DecentralizedNamespaceDefinition along with its authorized namespace owners in the form of NamespaceDelegations.<br/> May be provided, if so it must be fully authorized by the signatures in this request combined with the existing topology state.<br/> - A PartyToKeyMapping to register the party's signing keys.<br/> May be provided, if so it must be fully authorized by the signatures in this request combined with the existing topology state.<br/> - A PartyToParticipant to register the hosting relationship of the party.<br/> Must be provided.<br/> Required |  |
| 3 | `multi_hash_signatures` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.Signature` | repeated |  | Optional signatures of the combined hash of all onboarding_transactions<br/> This may be used instead of providing signatures on each individual transaction |  |
| 4 | `identity_provider_id` | `string` | optional |  | The id of the ``Identity Provider``<br/> If not set, assume the party is managed by the default identity provider.<br/> Optional |  |

| 2 | `update_mask` | `google.protobuf.FieldMask` | optional |  | An update mask specifies how and which properties of the ``PartyDetails`` message are to be updated.<br/> An update mask consists of a set of update paths.<br/> A valid update path points to a field or a subfield relative to the ``PartyDetails`` message.<br/> A valid update mask must:<br/><br/> 1. contain at least one update path,<br/> 2. contain only valid update paths.<br/><br/> Fields that can be updated are marked as ``Modifiable``.<br/> An update path can also point to non-``Modifiable`` fields such as 'party' and 'local_metadata.resource_version'<br/> because they are used:<br/><br/> 1. to identify the party details resource subject to the update,<br/> 2. for concurrent change control.<br/><br/> An update path can also point to non-``Modifiable`` fields such as 'is_local'<br/> as long as the values provided in the update request match the server values.<br/> Examples of update paths: 'local_metadata.annotations', 'local_metadata'.<br/> For additional information see the documentation for standard protobuf3's ``google.protobuf.FieldMask``.<br/> For similar Ledger API see ``com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.UpdateUserRequest``.<br/> Required |  |

#### Service `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.UserManagementService`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/user_management_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/user_management_service.proto#L43)
- Endpoints: 9

Service to manage users and their rights for interacting with the Ledger API
 served by a participant node.

 The authorization rules for its RPCs are specified on the ``&lt;RpcName&gt;Request``
 messages as boolean expressions over these facts:

 1. ``HasRight(r)`` denoting whether the authenticated user has right ``r`` and
 2. ``IsAuthenticatedUser(uid)`` denoting whether ``uid`` is the empty string or equal to the id of the authenticated user.
 3. ``IsAuthenticatedIdentityProviderAdmin(idp)`` denoting whether ``idp`` is equal to the ``identity_provider_id``
    of the authenticated user and the user has an IdentityProviderAdmin right.

 If `user_id` is set to the empty string (the default), then the data for the authenticated user will be retrieved.
 If `identity_provider_id` is set to an empty string, then it's effectively set to the value of access token's 'iss' field if that is provided.
 If `identity_provider_id` remains an empty string, the default identity provider will be assumed.

 The fields of request messages (and sub-messages) are marked either as ``Optional`` or ``Required``:

 1. ``Optional`` denoting the client may leave the field unset when sending a request.
 2. ``Required`` denoting the client must set the field to a non-default value when sending a request.

 A user resource consists of:

 1. a set of properties represented by the ``User`` message,
 2. a set of user rights, where each right is represented by the ``Right`` message.

 A user resource, once it has been created, can be modified.
 In order to update the properties represented by the ``User`` message use the ``UpdateUser`` RPC. The only fields that can be modified are those marked as ``Modifiable``.
 In order to grant or revoke user rights use ``GrantRights' and ``RevokeRights`` RPCs.

#### Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.User`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/user_management_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/admin/user_management_service.proto#L79)
- Fields: 5
- Nested messages: 0
- Nested enums: 0

Users and rights
/////////////////

Users are used to dynamically manage the rights given to Daml applications.
 They are stored and managed per participant node.

| # | Field | Type | Label | Oneof | Description | Metadata |
| -: | :---- | :--- | :---- | :---- | :---------- | :------- |
| 1 | `id` | `string` | optional |  | The user identifier, which must be a non-empty string of at most 128<br/> characters that are either alphanumeric ASCII characters or one of the symbols "@^$.!`-#+'~_\|:()".<br/> Required |  |
| 2 | `primary_party` | `string` | optional |  | The primary party as which this user reads and acts by default on the ledger<br/> *provided* it has the corresponding ``CanReadAs(primary_party)`` or<br/> ``CanActAs(primary_party)`` rights.<br/> Ledger API clients SHOULD set this field to a non-empty value for all users to<br/> enable the users to act on the ledger using their own Daml party.<br/> Users for participant administrators MAY have an associated primary party.<br/> Optional,<br/> Modifiable |  |
| 3 | `is_deactivated` | `bool` | optional |  | When set, then the user is denied all access to the Ledger API.<br/> Otherwise, the user has access to the Ledger API as per the user's rights.<br/> Optional,<br/> Modifiable |  |
| 4 | `metadata` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.ObjectMeta` | optional |  | The metadata of this user.<br/> Note that the ``metadata.resource_version`` tracks changes to the properties described by the ``User`` message and not the user's rights.<br/> Optional,<br/> Modifiable |  |
| 5 | `identity_provider_id` | `string` | optional |  | The ID of the identity provider configured by ``Identity Provider Config``<br/> Optional, if not set, assume the user is managed by the default identity provider. |  |

Oneofs:

  Required

| # | Field | Type | Label | Oneof | Description | Metadata |
| -: | :---- | :--- | :---- | :---- | :---------- | :------- |
| 1 | `participant_admin` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.Right.ParticipantAdmin` | optional | `kind` | The user can administer the participant node. |  |
| 2 | `can_act_as` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.Right.CanActAs` | optional | `kind` | The user can act as a specific party. |  |
| 3 | `can_read_as` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.Right.CanReadAs` | optional | `kind` | The user can read ledger data visible to a specific party. |  |
| 4 | `identity_provider_admin` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.Right.IdentityProviderAdmin` | optional | `kind` | The user can administer users and parties assigned to the same identity provider as the one of the user. |  |
| 5 | `can_read_as_any_party` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.Right.CanReadAsAnyParty` | optional | `kind` | The user can read as any party on a participant |  |
| 6 | `can_execute_as` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.Right.CanExecuteAs` | optional | `kind` | The user can prepare and execute submissions as a specific party. |  |
| 7 | `can_execute_as_any_party` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.Right.CanExecuteAsAnyParty` | optional | `kind` | The user can prepare and execute submissions as any party on a participant. |  |

 It means, being able to manage users and parties that are also assigned

No fields.

##### Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.Right.CanReadAsAnyParty`

- Nested messages: 0
- Nested enums: 0

The rights of a participant's super reader. Its utility is predominantly for
 feeding external tools, such as PQS, continually without the need to change subscriptions
 as new parties pop in and out of existence.

No fields.

| 2 | `update_mask` | `google.protobuf.FieldMask` | optional |  | An update mask specifies how and which properties of the ``User`` message are to be updated.<br/> An update mask consists of a set of update paths.<br/> A valid update path points to a field or a subfield relative to the ``User`` message.<br/> A valid update mask must:<br/><br/> 1. contain at least one update path,<br/> 2. contain only valid update paths.<br/><br/> Fields that can be updated are marked as ``Modifiable``.<br/> An update path can also point to a non-``Modifiable`` fields such as 'id' and 'metadata.resource_version'<br/> because they are used:<br/><br/> 1. to identify the user resource subject to the update,<br/> 2. for concurrent change control.<br/><br/> Examples of valid update paths: 'primary_party', 'metadata', 'metadata.annotations'.<br/> For additional information see the documentation for standard protobuf3's ``google.protobuf.FieldMask``.<br/> For similar Ledger API see ``com.daml.ledger.api.v2.admin.UpdatePartyDetailsRequest``.<br/> Required |  |

#### Service `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.interactive.InteractiveSubmissionService`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/interactive/interactive_submission_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/interactive/interactive_submission_service.proto#L31)
- Endpoints: 6

Service allowing interactive construction of command submissions

 The prepare and execute endpoints allow to submit commands in 2-steps:

 1. prepare transaction from commands,
 2. submit the prepared transaction

 This gives callers the ability to sign the daml transaction with their own signing keys

Execute a prepared submission _asynchronously_ on the ledger.
 Requires a signature of the transaction from the submitting external party.

##### Endpoint `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.interactive.InteractiveSubmissionService/ExecuteSubmissionAndWait`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/interactive/interactive_submission_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/interactive/interactive_submission_service.proto#L40)
- Endpoint page: [reference](/docs-main/appdev/reference/protobuf-history/endpoints/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/interactive/interactivesubmissionservice/executesubmissionandwait/index)
- Request: `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.interactive.ExecuteSubmissionAndWaitRequest`
- Response: `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.interactive.ExecuteSubmissionAndWaitResponse`
- Client streaming: no
- Server streaming: no

Similar to ExecuteSubmission but _synchronously_ wait for the completion of the transaction
 IMPORTANT: Relying on the response from this endpoint requires trusting the Participant Node to be honest.
 A malicious node could make a successfully committed request appeared failed and vice versa

Similar to ExecuteSubmissionAndWait but additionally returns the transaction
 IMPORTANT: Relying on the response from this endpoint requires trusting the Participant Node to be honest.
 A malicious node could make a successfully committed request appear as failed and vice versa

##### Endpoint `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.interactive.InteractiveSubmissionService/GetPreferredPackageVersion`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/interactive/interactive_submission_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/interactive/interactive_submission_service.proto#L58)
- Endpoint page: [reference](/docs-main/appdev/reference/protobuf-history/endpoints/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/interactive/interactivesubmissionservice/getpreferredpackageversion/index)
- Request: `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.interactive.GetPreferredPackageVersionRequest`
- Response: `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.interactive.GetPreferredPackageVersionResponse`
- Client streaming: no
- Server streaming: no

A preferred package is the highest-versioned package for a provided package-name
 that is vetted by all the participants hosting the provided parties.

 Ledger API clients should use this endpoint for constructing command submissions
 that are compatible with the provided preferred package, by making informed decisions on:
 - which are the compatible packages that can be used to create contracts
 - which contract or exercise choice argument version can be used in the command
 - which choices can be executed on a template or interface of a contract

 Can be accessed by any Ledger API client with a valid token when Ledger API authorization is enabled.

 Provided for backwards compatibility, it will be removed in the Canton version 3.4.0

##### Endpoint `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.interactive.InteractiveSubmissionService/GetPreferredPackages`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/interactive/interactive_submission_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/interactive/interactive_submission_service.proto#L76)
- Endpoint page: [reference](/docs-main/appdev/reference/protobuf-history/endpoints/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/interactive/interactivesubmissionservice/getpreferredpackages/index)
- Request: `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.interactive.GetPreferredPackagesRequest`
- Response: `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.interactive.GetPreferredPackagesResponse`
- Client streaming: no
- Server streaming: no

Compute the preferred packages for the vetting requirements in the request.
 A preferred package is the highest-versioned package for a provided package-name
 that is vetted by all the participants hosting the provided parties.

 Ledger API clients should use this endpoint for constructing command submissions
 that are compatible with the provided preferred packages, by making informed decisions on:
 - which are the compatible packages that can be used to create contracts
 - which contract or exercise choice argument version can be used in the command
 - which choices can be executed on a template or interface of a contract

 If the package preferences could not be computed due to no selection satisfying the requirements,
 a `FAILED_PRECONDITION` error will be returned.

 Can be accessed by any Ledger API client with a valid token when Ledger API authorization is enabled.

 Experimental API: this endpoint is not guaranteed to provide backwards compatibility in future releases

#### Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.interactive.CostEstimationHints`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/interactive/interactive_submission_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/interactive/interactive_submission_service.proto#L80)
- Fields: 2
- Nested messages: 0
- Nested enums: 0

Hints to improve cost estimation precision of a prepared transaction

| # | Field | Type | Label | Oneof | Description | Metadata |
| -: | :---- | :--- | :---- | :---- | :---------- | :------- |
| 1 | `disabled` | `bool` | optional |  | Disable cost estimation<br/> Default (not set) is false |  |
| 2 | `expected_signatures` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.SigningAlgorithmSpec` | repeated |  | Details on the keys that will be used to sign the transaction (how many and of which type).<br/> Signature size impacts the cost of the transaction.<br/> If empty, the signature sizes will be approximated with threshold-many signatures (where threshold is defined<br/> in the PartyToKeyMapping of the external party), using keys in the order they are registered.<br/> Optional (empty list is equivalent to not providing this field) |  |

#### Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.interactive.CostEstimation`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/interactive/interactive_submission_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/interactive/interactive_submission_service.proto#L96)
- Fields: 4
- Nested messages: 0
- Nested enums: 0

Estimation of the cost of submitting the prepared transaction
 The estimation is done against the synchronizer chosen during preparation of the transaction
 (or the one explicitly requested).
 The cost of re-assigning contracts to another synchronizer when necessary is not included in the estimation.

| # | Field | Type | Label | Oneof | Description | Metadata |
| -: | :---- | :--- | :---- | :---- | :---------- | :------- |
| 1 | `estimation_timestamp` | `google.protobuf.Timestamp` | optional |  | Timestamp at which the estimation was made |  |
| 2 | `confirmation_request_traffic_cost_estimation` | `uint64` | optional |  | Estimated traffic cost of the confirmation request associated with the transaction |  |
| 3 | `confirmation_response_traffic_cost_estimation` | `uint64` | optional |  | Estimated traffic cost of the confirmation response associated with the transaction<br/> This field can also be used as an indication of the cost that other potential confirming nodes<br/> of the party will incur to approve or reject the transaction |  |
| 4 | `total_traffic_cost_estimation` | `uint64` | optional |  | Sum of the fields above |  |

#### Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.interactive.PrepareSubmissionRequest`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/interactive/interactive_submission_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/interactive/interactive_submission_service.proto#L109)
- Fields: 13
- Nested messages: 0
- Nested enums: 0

_No description._

| # | Field | Type | Label | Oneof | Description | Metadata |
| -: | :---- | :--- | :---- | :---- | :---------- | :------- |
| 1 | `user_id` | `string` | optional |  | Uniquely identifies the participant user that prepares the transaction.<br/> Must be a valid UserIdString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> Required unless authentication is used with a user token.<br/> In that case, the token's user-id will be used for the request's user_id.<br/> Optional |  |
| 2 | `command_id` | `string` | optional |  | Uniquely identifies the command.<br/> The triple (user_id, act_as, command_id) constitutes the change ID for the intended ledger change,<br/> where act_as is interpreted as a set of party names.<br/> The change ID can be used for matching the intended ledger changes with all their completions.<br/> Must be a valid LedgerString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> Required |  |
| 3 | `commands` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.Command` | repeated |  | Individual elements of this atomic command. Must be non-empty.<br/> Limitation: Only single command transaction are currently supported by the API.<br/> The field is marked as repeated in preparation for future support of multiple commands.<br/> Required |  |
| 4 | `min_ledger_time` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.interactive.MinLedgerTime` | optional |  | Optional |  |
| 11 | `max_record_time` | `google.protobuf.Timestamp` | optional |  | Maximum timestamp at which the transaction can be recorded onto the ledger via the synchronizer specified in the `PrepareSubmissionResponse`.<br/> If submitted after it will be rejected even if otherwise valid, in which case it needs to be prepared and signed again<br/> with a new valid max_record_time.<br/> Use this to limit the time-to-life of a prepared transaction,<br/> which is useful to know when it can definitely not be accepted<br/> anymore and resorting to preparing another transaction for the same<br/> intent is safe again.<br/> Optional |  |
| 5 | `act_as` | `string` | repeated |  | Set of parties on whose behalf the command should be executed, if submitted.<br/> If ledger API authorization is enabled, then the authorization metadata must authorize the sender of the request<br/> to **read** (not act) on behalf of each of the given parties. This is because this RPC merely prepares a transaction<br/> and does not execute it. Therefore read authorization is sufficient even for actAs parties.<br/> Note: This may change, and more specific authorization scope may be introduced in the future.<br/> Each element must be a valid PartyIdString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> Required, must be non-empty. |  |
| 6 | `read_as` | `string` | repeated |  | Set of parties on whose behalf (in addition to all parties listed in ``act_as``) contracts can be retrieved.<br/> This affects Daml operations such as ``fetch``, ``fetchByKey``, ``lookupByKey``, ``exercise``, and ``exerciseByKey``.<br/> Note: A command can only use contracts that are visible to at least<br/> one of the parties in ``act_as`` or ``read_as``. This visibility check is independent from the Daml authorization<br/> rules for fetch operations.<br/> If ledger API authorization is enabled, then the authorization metadata must authorize the sender of the request<br/> to read contract data on behalf of each of the given parties.<br/> Optional |  |
| 7 | `disclosed_contracts` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.DisclosedContract` | repeated |  | Additional contracts used to resolve contract &amp; contract key lookups.<br/> Optional |  |
| 8 | `synchronizer_id` | `string` | optional |  | Must be a valid synchronizer id<br/> If not set, a suitable synchronizer that this node is connected to will be chosen<br/> Optional |  |
| 9 | `package_id_selection_preference` | `string` | repeated |  | The package-id selection preference of the client for resolving<br/> package names and interface instances in command submission and interpretation<br/> Optional |  |
| 10 | `verbose_hashing` | `bool` | optional |  | When true, the response will contain additional details on how the transaction was encoded and hashed<br/> This can be useful for troubleshooting of hash mismatches. Should only be used for debugging.<br/> Optional, default to false |  |
| 15 | `prefetch_contract_keys` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.PrefetchContractKey` | repeated |  | Fetches the contract keys into the caches to speed up the command processing.<br/> Should only contain contract keys that are expected to be resolved during interpretation of the commands.<br/> Keys of disclosed contracts do not need prefetching.<br/><br/> Optional |  |
| 16 | `estimate_traffic_cost` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.interactive.CostEstimationHints` | optional |  | Hints to improve the accuracy of traffic cost estimation.<br/> The estimation logic assumes that this node will be used for the execution of the transaction<br/> If another node is used instead, the estimation may be less precise.<br/> Request amplification is not accounted for in the estimation: each amplified request will<br/> result in the cost of the confirmation request to be charged additionally.<br/><br/> Optional - Traffic cost estimation is enabled by default if this field is not set<br/> To turn off cost estimation, set the CostEstimationHints#disabled field to true |  |

| # | Field | Type | Label | Oneof | Description | Metadata |
| -: | :---- | :--- | :---- | :---- | :---------- | :------- |
| 1 | `prepared_transaction` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.interactive.PreparedTransaction` | optional |  | The interpreted transaction, it represents the ledger changes necessary to execute the commands specified in the request.<br/> Clients MUST display the content of the transaction to the user for them to validate before signing the hash if the preparing participant is not trusted. |  |
| 2 | `prepared_transaction_hash` | `bytes` | optional |  | Hash of the transaction, this is what needs to be signed by the party to authorize the transaction.<br/> Only provided for convenience, clients MUST recompute the hash from the raw transaction if the preparing participant is not trusted.<br/> May be removed in future versions |  |
| 3 | `hashing_scheme_version` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.interactive.HashingSchemeVersion` | optional |  | The hashing scheme version used when building the hash |  |
| 4 | `hashing_details` | `string` | optional |  | Optional additional details on how the transaction was encoded and hashed. Only set if verbose_hashing = true in the request<br/> Note that there are no guarantees on the stability of the format or content of this field.<br/> Its content should NOT be parsed and should only be used for troubleshooting purposes. |  |
| 5 | `cost_estimation` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.interactive.CostEstimation` | optional |  | Traffic cost estimation of the prepared transaction<br/> Optional |  |

Oneofs:
- `deduplication_period` (2 fields)
  Specifies the deduplication period for the change ID (See PrepareSubmissionRequest).
 If omitted, the participant will assume the configured maximum deduplication time.
 Optional

| # | Field | Type | Label | Oneof | Description | Metadata |
| -: | :---- | :--- | :---- | :---- | :---------- | :------- |
| 1 | `prepared_transaction` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.interactive.PreparedTransaction` | optional |  | the prepared transaction<br/> Typically this is the value of the `prepared_transaction` field in `PrepareSubmissionResponse`<br/> obtained from calling `prepareSubmission`.<br/> Required |  |
| 2 | `party_signatures` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.interactive.PartySignatures` | optional |  | The party(ies) signatures that authorize the prepared submission to be executed by this node.<br/> Each party can provide one or more signatures..<br/> and one or more parties can sign.<br/> Note that currently, only single party submissions are supported.<br/> Required |  |
| 3 | `deduplication_duration` | `google.protobuf.Duration` | optional | `deduplication_period` | Specifies the length of the deduplication period.<br/> It is interpreted relative to the local clock at some point during the submission's processing.<br/> Must be non-negative. Must not exceed the maximum deduplication time. |  |
| 4 | `deduplication_offset` | `int64` | optional | `deduplication_period` | Specifies the start of the deduplication period by a completion stream offset (exclusive).<br/> Must be a valid absolute offset (positive integer). |  |
| 5 | `submission_id` | `string` | optional |  | A unique identifier to distinguish completions for different submissions with the same change ID.<br/> Typically a random UUID. Applications are expected to use a different UUID for each retry of a submission<br/> with the same change ID.<br/> Must be a valid LedgerString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/><br/> Required |  |
| 6 | `user_id` | `string` | optional |  | See [PrepareSubmissionRequest.user_id]<br/> Optional |  |
| 7 | `hashing_scheme_version` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.interactive.HashingSchemeVersion` | optional |  | The hashing scheme version used when building the hash<br/> Required |  |
| 8 | `min_ledger_time` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.interactive.MinLedgerTime` | optional |  | If set will influence the chosen ledger effective time but will not result in a submission delay so any override<br/> should be scheduled to executed within the window allowed by synchronizer.<br/> Optional |  |

| 4 | `deduplication_offset` | `int64` | optional | `deduplication_period` | Specifies the start of the deduplication period by a completion stream offset (exclusive).<br/> Must be a valid absolute offset (positive integer). |  |
| 5 | `submission_id` | `string` | optional |  | A unique identifier to distinguish completions for different submissions with the same change ID.<br/> Typically a random UUID. Applications are expected to use a different UUID for each retry of a submission<br/> with the same change ID.<br/> Must be a valid LedgerString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/><br/> Required |  |
| 6 | `user_id` | `string` | optional |  | See [PrepareSubmissionRequest.user_id]<br/> Optional |  |
| 7 | `hashing_scheme_version` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.interactive.HashingSchemeVersion` | optional |  | The hashing scheme version used when building the hash<br/> Required |  |
| 8 | `min_ledger_time` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.interactive.MinLedgerTime` | optional |  | If set will influence the chosen ledger effective time but will not result in a submission delay so any override<br/> should be scheduled to executed within the window allowed by synchronizer.<br/> Optional |  |

| 2 | `party_signatures` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.interactive.PartySignatures` | optional |  | The party(ies) signatures that authorize the prepared submission to be executed by this node.<br/> Each party can provide one or more signatures..<br/> and one or more parties can sign.<br/> Note that currently, only single party submissions are supported.<br/> Required |  |
| 3 | `deduplication_duration` | `google.protobuf.Duration` | optional | `deduplication_period` | Specifies the length of the deduplication period.<br/> It is interpreted relative to the local clock at some point during the submission's processing.<br/> Must be non-negative. Must not exceed the maximum deduplication time. |  |
| 4 | `deduplication_offset` | `int64` | optional | `deduplication_period` | Specifies the start of the deduplication period by a completion stream offset (exclusive).<br/> Must be a valid absolute offset (positive integer). |  |
| 5 | `submission_id` | `string` | optional |  | A unique identifier to distinguish completions for different submissions with the same change ID.<br/> Typically a random UUID. Applications are expected to use a different UUID for each retry of a submission<br/> with the same change ID.<br/> Must be a valid LedgerString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/><br/> Required |  |
| 6 | `user_id` | `string` | optional |  | See [PrepareSubmissionRequest.user_id]<br/> Optional |  |
| 7 | `hashing_scheme_version` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.interactive.HashingSchemeVersion` | optional |  | The hashing scheme version used when building the hash<br/> Required |  |
| 8 | `min_ledger_time` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.interactive.MinLedgerTime` | optional |  | If set will influence the chosen ledger effective time but will not result in a submission delay so any override<br/> should be scheduled to executed within the window allowed by synchronizer.<br/> Optional |  |
| 9 | `transaction_format` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.TransactionFormat` | optional |  | If no ``transaction_format`` is provided, a default will be used where ``transaction_shape`` is set to<br/> TRANSACTION_SHAPE_ACS_DELTA, ``event_format`` is defined with ``filters_by_party`` containing wildcard-template<br/> filter for all original ``act_as`` and ``read_as`` parties and the ``verbose`` flag is set.<br/> When the ``transaction_shape`` TRANSACTION_SHAPE_ACS_DELTA shape is used (explicitly or is defaulted to as explained above),<br/> events will only be returned if the submitting party is hosted on this node.<br/> Optional |  |

#### Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.interactive.MinLedgerTime`

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/interactive/interactive_submission_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/interactive/interactive_submission_service.proto#L426)

Oneofs:
- `time` (2 fields)

| # | Field | Type | Label | Oneof | Description | Metadata |
| -: | :---- | :--- | :---- | :---- | :---------- | :------- |
| 1 | `min_ledger_time_abs` | `google.protobuf.Timestamp` | optional | `time` | Lower bound for the ledger time assigned to the resulting transaction.<br/> The ledger time of a transaction is assigned as part of command interpretation.<br/> Important note: for interactive submissions, if the transaction depends on time, it **must** be signed<br/> and submitted within a time window around the ledger time assigned to the transaction during the prepare method.<br/> The time delta around that ledger time is a configuration of the ledger, usually short, around 1 minute.<br/> If however the transaction does not depend on time, the available time window to sign and submit the transaction is bound<br/> by the preparation time, which is also assigned in the "prepare" step (this request),<br/> but can be configured with a much larger skew, allowing for more time to sign the request (in the order of hours).<br/> Must not be set at the same time as min_ledger_time_rel.<br/> Optional |  |
| 2 | `min_ledger_time_rel` | `google.protobuf.Duration` | optional | `time` | Same as min_ledger_time_abs, but specified as a duration, starting from the time this request is received by the server.<br/> Must not be set at the same time as min_ledger_time_abs.<br/> Optional |  |

Transaction Metadata
 Refer to the hashing documentation for information on how it should be hashed.

| # | Field | Type | Label | Oneof | Description | Metadata |
| -: | :---- | :--- | :---- | :---- | :---------- | :------- |
| 2 | `submitter_info` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.interactive.Metadata.SubmitterInfo` | optional |  |  |  |
| 3 | `synchronizer_id` | `string` | optional |  |  |  |
| 4 | `mediator_group` | `uint32` | optional |  |  |  |
| 5 | `transaction_uuid` | `string` | optional |  |  |  |
| 6 | `preparation_time` | `uint64` | optional |  |  |  |
| 7 | `input_contracts` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.interactive.Metadata.InputContract` | repeated |  |  |  |
| 9 | `min_ledger_effective_time` | `uint64` | optional |  | Where ledger time constraints are imposed during the execution of the contract they will be populated<br/> in the fields below. These are optional because if the transaction does NOT depend on time, these values<br/> do not need to be set.<br/> The final ledger effective time used will be chosen when the command is submitted through the [execute] RPC.<br/> If the ledger effective time is outside of any populated min/max bounds then a different transaction<br/> can result, that will cause a confirmation message rejection. |  |
| 10 | `max_ledger_effective_time` | `uint64` | optional |  |  |  |
| 8 | `global_key_mapping` | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.interactive.Metadata.GlobalKeyMappingEntry` | repeated |  | **********************************************************<br/><br/>** Metadata information that does NOT need to be signed **<br/><br/>**********************************************************<br/><br/>Contextual information needed to process the transaction but not signed, either because it's already indirectly<br/> signed by signing the transaction, or because it doesn't impact the ledger state |  |
| 11 | `max_record_time` | `uint64` | optional |  | Maximum timestamp at which the transaction can be recorded onto the ledger via the synchronizer `synchronizer_id`.<br/> If submitted after it will be rejected even if otherwise valid, in which case it needs to be prepared and signed again<br/> with a new valid max_record_time.<br/> Unsigned in 3.3 to avoid a breaking protocol change<br/> Will be signed in 3.4+<br/> Set max_record_time in the PreparedTransactionRequest to get this field set accordingly |  |
